summary: The session/open and session/close OTL builders are now tied directly to login/session root fields, the root active request slot, and the shared 0x14062BCE0 submitter contract. This pass also resolves the close-builder indirect CALL R10 sites as small-string heap cleanup calls for temporary endpoint/body strings, not transport dispatch calls.
confidence: high for endpoint literals, request allocation/initializer/submitter, root +0x68 request-slot replacement, request +0x224 = 5, submitter argument roles, and post-submit string cleanup; medium-high for the root-field semantic names; medium for the exact close-duration timebase math.

logical_builders.role: Builds a session/open request, replaces the active root request slot at root +0x68, and submits a JSON-ish empty body through 0x14062BCE0.

caller.rdx: stack-composed session-open path component from the caller

key_actions: Checks the root string-like field at +0x50/+0x5F before proceeding.
If root +0x68 already holds an active request, calls 0x14062B0B0 to drain/reset outstanding work before replacing the slot.
Formats session/open/%s using the caller-provided path component.
Conditionally appends ?userHash=%s using string-like material selected through the root +0x30/+0x47 path.
Allocates 0x268 bytes, initializes the request through 0x14062B7B0, swaps it into root +0x68, cleans/frees any replaced request, writes request +0x224 = 5, and calls 0x14062BCE0.

logical_builders.role: Builds a session/close request from the login/session root, replaces the active root request slot, submits through 0x14062BCE0, and cleans temporary strings.

entry_model.reason: The builder is a logical fall-through continuation of the close/flush coordinator body after the pending-entry drain range, rather than a standalone externally called function.

key_actions: Computes a close/session duration value, subtracting root +0x60 from the converted time value before formatting the query.
Formats session/close/%s/%s from root string-like fields at +0x00/+0x17, +0x18/+0x2F, and +0x48/+0x5F.
Appends either ?sessionDuration=%d or ?userHash=%s&sessionDuration=%d depending on whether root +0x30/+0x47 userHash material is present.
Allocates and initializes a 0x268 request object, swaps it into the active request slot carried in RSI, cleans/frees any replaced request, writes request +0x224 = 5, and calls 0x14062BCE0.
After submission, releases heap-backed temporary body and endpoint strings through the indirect R10 deallocator path.

root_field_flow.role: String-like close endpoint component copied into the close endpoint stack string.
evidence: 0x14062ACC4 and 0x14062ACC8 select heap storage through [root+0x8]/[root] when the sentinel high bit is set.
0x14062ACE1 copies the selected string into the close endpoint stack string.

root_field_flow.role: String-like path component used by session/close and already shared with pending event endpoint formatting.
evidence: 0x14062ACFC loads root+0x18 as the second formatted path component for session/close/%s/%s.

root_field_flow.role: Optional userHash string-like field for session/open and session/close query strings.
evidence: 0x14062A9E2 checks [root+0x47] before appending ?userHash=%s in the open builder.
0x14062AD1B checks [root+0x47] before choosing between the close duration-only query and the userHash+duration query.
0x14062AD4E loads root+0x30 as the userHash source when the query includes userHash.

root_field_flow.role: String-like close endpoint component used in session/close/%s/%s.
evidence: 0x14062ACEA loads root+0x48 as an argument to session/close/%s/%s.

root_field_flow.role: Session start or previous timestamp value subtracted from the close-time value to form sessionDuration.
evidence: 0x14062ACAD subtracts [root+0x60] from EBX before the duration query is formatted.
0x14062AD38 and 0x14062AD59 pass EBX as the %d sessionDuration argument.

root_field_flow.role: Active 0x268-byte session request slot used by session/open and session/close.
evidence: 0x14062A7AC checks [root+0x68] and 0x14062A7B1/0x14062A7C1 compute root+0x68 as the replacement slot.
0x14062AA4A stores the new open request into the slot and 0x14062AD9F stores the new close request into the close path slot carried in RSI.
Both builders call 0x14062B8C0 and 0x1407B8478 on replaced request pointers.

submitter_contract.rdx: stack-composed endpoint string at RBP-0x20, selected through short/heap sentinel
submitter_contract.r8: stack-composed body string at RBP-0x38, initialized from literal {}

submitter_contract.rcx: new request from active slot pointer in RSI
submitter_contract.rdx: stack-composed endpoint string at RSP+0x28, selected through short/heap sentinel
submitter_contract.r8: stack-composed body string at RSP+0x40, initialized from literal {}

evidence: The calls are gated by the high bit of the stack string sentinel bytes at RSP+0x57 and RSP+0x3F.
The pointer to release is loaded from the stack string storage only when the sentinel indicates heap backing.

The path loads an allocator/string service from [0x140E57B40], selects a backing pool through +0x100/+0x108 or +0x198, selects a size/count through +0x1A0/+0x1A8 or +0x1F8, loads a vtable slot at [pool_vtable+0x20], and calls R10 with RCX=pool, RDX=pointer, R8D=size.
The same cleanup shape appears in the session/open builder, reinforcing this as temporary string cleanup rather than request transport dispatch.

opennxt_947_server_implications: The session open/close requests are ordinary OTL transport submissions with endpoint strings and literal {} bodies, so OpenNXT can model them through the same request pipeline as auth and pending telemetry.

The client records an active request at root +0x68 for both open and close. Replacement drains or frees the prior request, so tests should not assume multiple session open/close requests coexist in that slot.
The close request computes and sends sessionDuration from client-side timing state. Server traces should expect close URLs to include duration even when userHash is absent.
The indirect R10 sites after close submit are allocator cleanup for heap-backed temporary endpoint/body strings. They do not represent a second network submit or callback dispatch.

reverser_tool_update.reason: The existing Reverser instruction, literal, field-reference, branch-target, and callsite-register commands were enough to close this milestone.
reverser_tool_update.next_native_capability_candidate: A recognizer for small-string sentinel cleanup plus allocator vtable free calls would let future summaries label indirect CALL R10 cleanup sites automatically.

next_targets: Map the root +0x60 producer to prove the exact sessionDuration timebase.
Resolve the string/path producers for root +0x18, +0x30, +0x48, and +0x50.
Trace the remaining session/open indirect calls around 0x14062A88D, 0x14062A89A, 0x14062A8F1, and 0x14062AB44 to separate callback dispatch from temporary string cleanup.
Promote small-string cleanup recognition into Reverser if the same allocator pattern appears in another request family.
